strengths: ~20 years of hands-on engineering spanning web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput distributed systems, satisfying the experience threshold
Demonstrated entrepreneurial initiative: founded and maintains GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform used in production by real organizations
Recent engagement with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq signals active pivot toward the AI space
concerns: AI depth is extremely shallow and very recent (2 months at re:cinq); no demonstrated AI products, platforms, or research history required for this director-level IC role
No advanced degree (Master's or PhD) in an AI-related field, and no evidence of recognized external AI expertise such as publications, keynotes, or advisory roles
Current and historical titles (Staff/Senior Software Developer) reflect an individual execution track, not the strategic, customer-advisory, and cross-org influence expected at director-level
response.justification: James Smith brings genuine breadth across the engineering stack and a founder mindset, but the role demands a recognized AI authority who can set technical strategy, advise enterprise customers, and influence at a director level — none of which his profile substantiates. His AI work amounts to roughly two months of MCP tooling, with no prior AI product history, no advanced AI credentials, and no evidence of the external thought-leadership or customer-advisory experience the position requires. The experience volume is present, but the depth, seniority tier, and AI specialization that define this role are materially absent.
